title: Coelogyne lepantoensis
intro: Coelogyne lepantoensis, also known as Lepanto Coelogyne, is an epiphytic orchid species native to the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne lepantoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne lepantoensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of ointments and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Coelogyne lepantoensis is classified as an orchid species within the family Orchidaceae. Coelogyne lepantoensis is further classified as Coelogyne lepantoensis (Ames) Ames, indicating its specific epithet and classification within the genus.
